field.name: name
field.placeholder: e.g. Create Quotation, Update Stage, Send Notification

field.name: model_id
field.help: The Odoo model this action operates on, e.g. sale.order, crm.lead. Use ai.agent for actions spanning multiple models.

field.name: use_in_ai
field.help: Check this to make the action available in AI Topics and agents.

field.name: ai_tool_allow_end_message
field.help: When checked, the AI stops calling further tools after this action and shows its final reply to the user. Use for 'last step' actions.

page.name: description
page.div: Tell the LLM what this tool does and when to use it. Be specific — this is what the AI reads to decide whether to call this action.

field.name: ai_tool_description
field.placeholder:
Example:
Creates a sales quotation for a customer. Automatically finds or creates the customer and products by name.
Use when the user says: 'create a quote', 'make an offer', 'prepare an order for [customer]'.
Returns the new quotation name (e.g. S00042) on success.

page.name: schema
page.div: Define the parameters the LLM will pass to this action. Each property in the schema becomes a local variable in your Python code. Leave empty if no parameters are needed.

field.name: ai_tool_schema

ul.li: record / records — current record(s) if record-bound
ai['result'] — set this to return a message back to the LLM
All schema properties are injected as local variables (e.g. customer_name, product_lines)
Find-or-create: search first, create if empty

field.name: code
help
field.p: AI Actions are Python functions the AI agent can call to perform tasks — like creating a quotation, updating a record, or sending a notification.
Each action needs a name, a description (so the AI knows when to use it), a parameters schema (JSON), and the Python code to execute.
